core_mechanism: A topology-control-parameter-driven bifurcation on a network interpolating between a regular lattice and a random graph: as the fraction of long-range/random links crosses a critical value, a noisy stationary state loses stability and gives way to a self-sustained global limit-cycle oscillation (Hopf-type onset of collective, phase-locked oscillation).
brief_reason: The abstract reports a network tuned from ordered lattices to random graphs where, at a finite value of the disorder, the fluctuating low-level stationary state gives way to self-sustained oscillations in the infected fraction, i.e. a connectivity-parameter-driven transition from a fixed point to a sustained collective oscillation.
